contents: (** This is part of the formalization of section 4 of the paper: Injective Types in Univalent Mathematics by Martín Escardó.  Many proofs are guided by Martín Escardó's original Agda formalization of this paper which can be found at: https://www.cs.bham.ac.uk/~mhe/TypeTopology/InjectiveTypes.Article.html. *)
Require Import Types.Sigma Types.Unit Types.Arrow Types.Forall Types.Empty Types.Universe Types.Equiv Types.Paths.

(** We are careful about universe variables for these first few definitions because they are used in the rest of the paper.  We use [u], [v], [w], etc. as our typical universe variables. Our convention for the max of two universes [u] and [v] is [uv]. *)
Universes u v w uv uw vw uvw.

Constraint u <= uv, v <= uv, u <= uw, w <= uw, v <= vw, w <= vw,
    uv <= uvw, uw <= uvw, vw <= uvw.

(** Below we will introduce notations [P <| j] and [P |> j] for these Kan extensions. *)

  (** If [j] is an embedding, then [P <| j] and [P |> j] are extensions in the following sense: [(P <| j o j) x <~> P x <~> (P |> j o j) x].  So, with univalence, we get that they are extensions. *)

goals.conclusion: LeftKanFam P j (j x) <~> P x
LeftKanFam P j (j x) <~> P x
RightKanFam P j (j x) <~> P x
RightKanFam P j (j x) <~> P x

contents:
Definition isext_leftkanfam@{suvw | uvw < suvw} `{Univalence} {X : Type@{u}} {Y : Type@{v}}
    (P : X -> Type@{w}) (j : X -> Y) (isem : IsEmbedding@{u v uv} j) (x : X)
    : @paths@{suvw} Type@{uvw} (LeftKanFam@{} P j (j x)) (P x)
    := path_universe_uncurried (isext_equiv_leftkanfam _ _ _ _).

Definition isext_rightkanfam@{suvw | uvw < suvw} `{Univalence} {X : Type@{u}} {Y : Type@{v}}
    (P : X -> Type@{w}) (j : X -> Y) (isem : IsEmbedding@{u v uv} j) (x : X)
    : @paths@{suvw} Type@{uvw} (RightKanFam@{} P j (j x)) (P x)
    := path_universe_uncurried (isext_equiv_rightkanfam _ _ _ _).
(** For [y : Y] not in the image of [j], [(P <| j) y] is empty and [(P |> j) y] is contractible. *)

Definition isempty_leftkanfam {X : Type} {Y : Type}
  (P : X -> Type) (j : X -> Y) (y : Y) (ynin : forall x : X, j x <> y)
  : (P <| j) y -> Empty
  := fun '((x; p); _) => ynin x p.

goals.conclusion: Contr ((P |> j) y)

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, j x <> y

goals.conclusion: Contr ((P |> j) y)

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, j x <> y

goals.conclusion: forall a : hfiber j y, Contr ((fun a0 : hfiber j y => P a0.1) a)

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, j x <> y

goals.conclusion: Contr (P (x; p).1)

hypotheses.type: forall x0 : X, j x0 <> y

contents: (** The following equivalences tell us that [{ y : Y & (P <| j) y}] and [forall y : Y, (P |> j) y] can be thought of as just different notation for the sum and product of a type family. *)

goals.conclusion: ?f o ?g == idmap
?g o ?f == idmap

contents: exact (fun w : {x : X & P x} => (j w.1; (w.1; idpath); w.2)).

goals.conclusion:
(fun w : {x : X & P x} => (j w.1; (w.1; 1); w.2))
o (fun pat : {y : Y & (P <| j) y} =>
   let x := pat in
   let y := x.1 in
   let proj2 := x.2 in
   let proj0 := proj2 in
   let proj1 := proj0.1 in
   let y' := proj0.2 in
   (let proj3 := proj1 in
    let x0 := proj3.1 in
    let p := proj3.2 in fun y'0 : P (x0; p).1 => (x0; y'0)) y') ==
idmap

(fun pat : {y : Y & (P <| j) y} =>
 let x := pat in
 let y := x.1 in
 let proj2 := x.2 in
 let proj0 := proj2 in
 let proj1 := proj0.1 in
 let y' := proj0.2 in
 (let proj3 := proj1 in
  let x0 := proj3.1 in let p := proj3.2 in fun y'0 : P (x0; p).1 => (x0; y'0))
   y')
o (fun w : {x : X & P x} => (j w.1; (w.1; 1); w.2)) == idmap
(forall x : X, P x) <~> (forall y : Y, (P |> j) y)
(forall x : X, P x) <~> (forall y : Y, (P |> j) y)
(forall x : X, P x) -> forall y : Y, (P |> j) y
(forall y : Y, (P |> j) y) -> forall x : X, P x
?f o ?g == idmap
?g o ?f == idmap
(forall x : X, P x) -> forall y : Y, (P |> j) y
P w.1
(forall y : Y, (P |> j) y) -> forall x : X, P x

contents: exact (fun h x => h (j x) (x; idpath)).

goals.conclusion:
(fun (g : forall x : X, P x) (y : Y) =>
 (fun w : hfiber j y => g w.1) : (P |> j) y)
o (fun (h : forall y : Y, (P |> j) y) (x : X) => h (j x) (x; 1)) == idmap
(fun (y : Y) (w : hfiber j y) => h (j w.1) (w.1; 1)) = h

(fun (h : forall y : Y, (P |> j) y) (x : X) => h (j x) (x; 1))
o (fun (g : forall x : X, P x) (y : Y) =>
   (fun w : hfiber j y => g w.1) : (P |> j) y) ==
idmap
(fun x : X => g (x; 1).1) = g

contents:
(** Here we are taking the perspective of a type family [P : X -> Type] as an oo-presheaf, considering the interpretation of [X] as an oo-groupoid and [Type] as a universe of spaces i.e. an appropriate generalization of the category of sets. It is easy to see that a type family [P] is functorial if we define its action on paths with [transport]. Functoriality then reduces to known lemmas about the [transport] function. *)

(** With this in mind, we define the type of transformations between two type families. [concat_Ap] says that these transformations are automatically natural. *)

Definition MapFam {X : Type} (P : X -> Type) (R : X -> Type)
  := forall x, P x -> R x.

Definition compose_mapfam {X} {P R Q : X -> Type} (b : R >=> Q) (a : P >=> R)
  : P >=> Q
  := fun x => (b x) o (a x).
(** If [j] is an embedding then [(P <| j) =< (P |> j)]. *)

goals.conclusion: P w.1
w' = w

contents: (** Under this interpretation, we can think of the maps [P <| j] and [P |> j] as left and right Kan extensions of [P : X -> Type] along [j : X -> Y]. To see this we can construct the (co)unit transformations of our extensions. *)

Definition unit_leftkanfam {X Y : Type} (P : X -> Type) (j : X -> Y)
  : P >=> ((P <| j) o j)
  := fun x A => ((x; idpath); A).

Definition counit_rightkanfam {X Y : Type} (P : X -> Type) (j : X -> Y)
  : ((P |> j) o j) >=> P
  := fun x A => A (x; idpath).

Definition counit_leftkanfam {X Y : Type} (R : Y -> Type) (j : X -> Y)
  : ((R o j) <| j) >=> R.

goals.conclusion: (R o j) <| j >=> R
(R o j) <| j >=> R
R y

contents:
Definition unit_rightkanfam {X Y : Type} (R : Y -> Type) (j : X -> Y)
  : R >=> ((R o j) |> j).

goals.conclusion: R >=> (R o j) |> j
R >=> (R o j) |> j

contents: (** Universal property of the Kan extensions. *)

goals.conclusion: {b : P <| j >=> R & compose_mapfam (b o j) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a}
{b : P <| j >=> R & compose_mapfam (b o j) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a}

(fun b : P <| j >=> R => compose_mapfam (b o j) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a)
  ?proj1
R y

(fun b : P <| j >=> R => compose_mapfam (b o j) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a)
  ((fun (y : Y) (X0 : (P <| j) y) =>
    (fun proj1 : hfiber j y =>
     (fun (x : X) (p : j x = y) (A : P (x; p).1) => transport R p (a x A))
       proj1.1 proj1.2)
      X0.1 X0.2)
   :
   P <| j >=> R)

compose_mapfam
  (fun (x0 : X) (X0 : (P <| j) (j x0)) =>
   transport R (X0.1).2 (a (X0.1).1 X0.2))
  (unit_leftkanfam P j) x =
a x
Contr {b : P <| j >=> R & compose_mapfam (b o j) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a}
Contr {b : P <| j >=> R & compose_mapfam (b o j) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a}

forall
y : {b : P <| j >=> R &
    compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a},
univ_property_leftkanfam a = y
univ_property_leftkanfam a = (b; F)

hypotheses.type: compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a

goals.conclusion: (b; F) = univ_property_leftkanfam a

hypotheses.type: compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a

contents: (* Do now to avoid inversion in the first subgoal. *)

goals.conclusion: (b; F).1 = (univ_property_leftkanfam a).1

hypotheses.type: compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a

goals.conclusion:
transport
  (fun b0 : P <| j >=> R =>
   compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b0 (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a)
  ?p (b; F).2 =
(univ_property_leftkanfam a).2

hypotheses.type: compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a

goals.conclusion: (b; F).1 = (univ_property_leftkanfam a).1

hypotheses.type: compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a

goals.conclusion: (b; F).1 y = (univ_property_leftkanfam a).1 y

hypotheses.type: compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a

goals.conclusion: (b; F).1 (j w) ((w; 1); c) = (univ_property_leftkanfam a).1 (j w) ((w; 1); c)

hypotheses.type: compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a

goals.conclusion:
transport
  (fun b0 : P <| j >=> R =>
   compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b0 (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a)
  (path_forall (b; F).1 (univ_property_leftkanfam a).1
     ((fun y : Y =>
       path_forall ((b; F).1 y) ((univ_property_leftkanfam a).1 y)
         ((fun x : (P <| j) y =>
           (fun proj1 : hfiber j y =>
            (fun (w : X) (proj2 : j w = y) =>
             match
               proj2 as p in (_ = y0)
               return
                 (forall proj0 : P (w; p).1,
                  (b; F).1 y0 ((w; p); proj0) =
                  (univ_property_leftkanfam a).1 y0 ((w; p); proj0))
             with
             | 1 => fun c : P (w; 1).1 => ap10 (F w) c
             end) proj1.1 proj1.2) x.1 x.2)
          :
          (b; F).1 y == (univ_property_leftkanfam a).1 y))
      :
      (b; F).1 == (univ_property_leftkanfam a).1))
  (b; F).2 =
(univ_property_leftkanfam a).2

hypotheses.type: compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a

goals.conclusion:
transport
  (fun b0 : P <| j >=> R =>
   compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b0 (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a)
  (path_forall b
     (fun (y : Y) (X0 : (P <| j) y) => transport R (X0.1).2 (a (X0.1).1 X0.2))
     (fun y : Y =>
      path_forall (b y)
        (fun X0 : (P <| j) y => transport R (X0.1).2 (a (X0.1).1 X0.2))
        (fun x : (P <| j) y =>
         match
           (x.1).2 as p in (_ = y0)
           return
             (forall proj2 : P (x.1).1,
              b y0 (((x.1).1; p); proj2) = transport R p (a (x.1).1 proj2))
         with
         | 1 => fun c : P (x.1).1 => ap10 (F (x.1).1) c
         end x.2)))
  F =
(fun x : X => 1)

hypotheses.type: compose_mapfam (fun x : X => b (j x)) (unit_leftkanfam P j) == a

transport
  (fun b0 : P <| j >=> R =>

  F x =
1

transport

ap (fun (ka : (P <| j) (j x) -> R (j x)) (x0 : P x) => ka (i x0))
  (ap (fun k : P <| j >=> R => k (j x))
     (path_forall b
        (fun (y : Y) (X0 : (P <| j) y) =>
         transport R (X0.1).2 (a (X0.1).1 X0.2))
        (fun y : Y =>
         path_forall (b y)
           (fun X0 : (P <| j) y => transport R (X0.1).2 (a (X0.1).1 X0.2))
           (fun x0 : (P <| j) y =>
            match
              (x0.1).2 as p in (_ = y0)

                 transport R p (a (x0.1).1 proj2))
            with
            | 1 => fun c : P (x0.1).1 => ap10 (F (x0.1).1) c
            end x0.2)))) =
F x

goals.conclusion:
ap (fun (ka : (P <| j) (j x) -> R (j x)) (x0 : P x) => ka (i x0))
  (path_forall (b (j x))
     (fun X0 : (P <| j) (j x) => transport R (X0.1).2 (a (X0.1).1 X0.2))
     (fun x0 : (P <| j) (j x) =>
      match
        (x0.1).2 as p in (_ = y)
        return
          (forall proj2 : P (x0.1).1,
           b y (((x0.1).1; p); proj2) = transport R p (a (x0.1).1 proj2))
      with
      | 1 => fun c : P (x0.1).1 => ap10 (F (x0.1).1) c
      end x0.2)) =
F x

contents: (** The above (co)unit constructions are special cases of the following, which tells us that these extensions are adjoint to restriction by [j] *)

Definition leftadjoint_leftkanfam `{Funext} {X Y : Type} (P : X -> Type)
  (R : Y -> Type) (j : X -> Y)
  : ((P <| j) >=> R) <~> (P >=> R o j).

goals.conclusion: P <| j >=> R -> P >=> R o j

  : (R >=> (P |> j)) <~> (R o j >=> P).

contents: (** This section is all set up for the proof that the left Kan extension of an embedding is an embedding of type families. *)
Context `{Univalence} {X Y : Type} (j : X -> Y) (isem : IsEmbedding j).

(** The counit transformation of a type family of the form [P <| j] has each of its components an equivalence.  In other words, the type family [P <| j] is the left Kan extension of its restriction along [j]. *)

Definition isequiv_counit_leftkanfam_leftkanfam (P : X -> Type) {y : Y}
    : IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam (P <| j) j y).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam (P <| j) j y)
IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam (P <| j) j y)
counit_leftkanfam (P <| j) j y o ?g == idmap
?g o counit_leftkanfam (P <| j) j y == idmap

contents: exact (fun '(((x; p); C) : (P <| j) y) => ((x; p); ((x; idpath); C))).

goals.conclusion:
counit_leftkanfam (P <| j) j y
o (fun pat : (P <| j) y =>
   let x := pat in
   let proj1 := x.1 in
   let C := x.2 in
   (let proj0 := proj1 in
    let x0 := proj0.1 in
    let p := proj0.2 in fun C0 : P (x0; p).1 => ((x0; p); (x0; 1); C0)) C) ==
idmap

(fun pat : (P <| j) y =>
 let x := pat in
 let proj1 := x.1 in
 let C := x.2 in
 (let proj0 := proj1 in
  let x0 := proj0.1 in
  let p := proj0.2 in fun C0 : P (x0; p).1 => ((x0; p); (x0; 1); C0)) C)
o counit_leftkanfam (P <| j) j y == idmap

contents: intros [[x []] [[x' p'] C]]; cbn; cbn in C, p'.

goals.conclusion: forall x0 : x' = x, ((x'; ap j x0); (x'; 1); C) = ((x; 1); (x'; ap j x0); C)

contents: (** We now use the above fact to show that type families over [X] are equivalent to type families over [Y] such that the counit map is a natural isomorphism. *)

Definition leftkanfam_counit_equiv (P : X -> Type)
    : { R : Y -> Type & forall y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y) }.

goals.conclusion: {R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)}
{R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)}
forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam (P <| j) j y)
IsEquiv leftkanfam_counit_equiv
IsEquiv leftkanfam_counit_equiv

{R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)} ->
X -> Type
leftkanfam_counit_equiv o ?g == idmap
?g o leftkanfam_counit_equiv == idmap

{R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)} ->
X -> Type
X -> Type

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)

goals.conclusion:
leftkanfam_counit_equiv
o (fun X0 : {R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)} =>
   (fun (R : Y -> Type) (_ : forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)) =>
    R o j) X0.1 X0.2) ==
idmap
leftkanfam_counit_equiv (fun x : X => R (j x)) = (R; e)

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)

goals.conclusion: (fun x : X => R (j x)) <| j = R

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)

goals.conclusion: ((fun x : X => R (j x)) <| j) y = R y

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y0)

goals.conclusion:
(fun X0 : {R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)} =>
 (fun (R : Y -> Type) (_ : forall y : Y, IsEquiv (counit_leftkanfam R j y)) =>
  R o j) X0.1 X0.2)
o leftkanfam_counit_equiv == idmap
(fun x : X => (leftkanfam_counit_equiv P).1 (j x)) = P
(leftkanfam_counit_equiv P).1 (j x) = P x

contents: (** Using these facts we can show that the map [_ <| j] is an embedding if [j] is an embedding. *)

Definition isembed_leftkanfam_ext
    : IsEmbedding (fun P => P <| j)
    := mapinO_compose (O:=-1) leftkanfam_counit_equiv pr1.
(** Dual proof for the right Kan extension. *)
Context `{Univalence} {X Y : Type} (j : X -> Y) (isem : IsEmbedding j).

Definition isequiv_unit_rightkanfam_rightkanfam (P : X -> Type) {y : Y}
    : IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y)
IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y)
unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y o ?g == idmap
?g o unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y == idmap

unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y
o (fun C : ((P |> j o j) |> j) y =>
   (fun w : hfiber j y =>
    (fun (x : X) (p : j x = y) => C (x; p) (x; 1)) w.1 w.2)
   :
   (P |> j) y) ==
idmap

unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y (fun w : hfiber j y => C (w.1; w.2) (w.1; 1)) =
C

hypotheses.type: ((fun x : X => (P |> j) (j x)) |> j) y

goals.conclusion:
unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y (fun w : hfiber j y => C (w.1; w.2) (w.1; 1))
  (x; p) =
C (x; p)

unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j (j x)
  (fun w : hfiber j (j x) => C (w.1; w.2) (w.1; 1)) 
  (x; 1) =
C (x; 1)

unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j (j x)
  (fun w0 : hfiber j (j x) => C (w0.1; w0.2) (w0.1; 1)) 
  (x; 1) w =
C (x; 1) w

contents:
rapply (@transport _ (fun t => C t (t.1; idpath) = C (x; idpath) t) _ w
        (center _ (isem (j x) (x; idpath) w)) idpath).

goals.conclusion:
(fun C : ((P |> j o j) |> j) y =>
 (fun w : hfiber j y =>
  (fun (x : X) (p : j x = y) => C (x; p) (x; 1)) w.1 w.2)
 :
 (P |> j) y)
o unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y == idmap

(fun w : hfiber j y => unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y a (w.1; w.2) (w.1; 1)) =
a
unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y a (x; p) (x; 1) = a (x; p)
unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j (j x) a (x; 1) (x; 1) = a (x; 1)

Definition rightkanfam_unit_equiv (P : X -> Type)
    : {R : Y -> Type & forall y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)}.

goals.conclusion: {R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)}
{R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)}
forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam (P |> j) j y)
IsEquiv rightkanfam_unit_equiv
IsEquiv rightkanfam_unit_equiv
{R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)} -> X -> Type
rightkanfam_unit_equiv o ?g == idmap
?g o rightkanfam_unit_equiv == idmap

X -> Type

goals.conclusion:
rightkanfam_unit_equiv
o (fun X0 : {R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)} =>
   (fun (R : Y -> Type) (_ : forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)) =>
    R o j) X0.1 X0.2) ==
idmap
rightkanfam_unit_equiv (fun x : X => R (j x)) = (R; e)

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)

goals.conclusion: (fun x : X => R (j x)) |> j = R

hypotheses.type: forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)

goals.conclusion: ((fun x : X => R (j x)) |> j) y = R y

hypotheses.type: forall y0 : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y0)

goals.conclusion:
(fun X0 : {R : Y -> Type & forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)} =>
 (fun (R : Y -> Type) (_ : forall y : Y, IsEquiv (unit_rightkanfam R j y)) =>
  R o j) X0.1 X0.2)
o rightkanfam_unit_equiv == idmap
(fun x : X => (rightkanfam_unit_equiv P).1 (j x)) = P
(rightkanfam_unit_equiv P).1 (j x) = P x

contents: (** The map [_ |> j] is an embedding if [j] is an embedding. *)

Definition isembed_rightkanfam_ext
    : IsEmbedding (fun P => P |> j)
    := mapinO_compose (O:=-1) rightkanfam_unit_equiv pr1.
